title: Transcript:Wanted!
wikitext:
{{Transcript|quest}}
{{Transcript list|Sir Tiffy Cashien|Sir Amik Varze|Savant|Solus Dellagar|Lord Daquarius|Mage of Zamorak|Woman (Wanted!)|Black Knight (Wanted!)}}

==Starting the Quest==
===Before the quest is accepted===
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' What ho, [sirrah/milady]. Jolly good show on the old training grounds thingy, what?
* {{topt|Do you have any jobs for me yet?}}
** '''Player:''' So Tiffy, now that I'm a Temple Knight Initiate do you have any jobs for me to do?
** '''Player:''' Do you have any quests for a hard-working adventurer?
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well old [boy/gal/bean], looking at the old dossier we have on you here there seems to be a tiny problem, what?
** '''Player:''' Problem? What problem? Er... and you have a dossier on me???
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well OF COURSE we have a dossier on you! We have a dossier on EVERYONE! It's our job, dontchaknow!
** '''Player:''' Um... okay... So what's the problem?
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well now, seems we made a tiny blunder when we first contacted you to join the Temple Knights, what?
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Entirely our own fault, could have been avoided and all that, but a bit of a show-stopper, what?
** '''Player:''' What is?
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well [lad/lass], here's the thing: Only members of the White Knights may join the Temple Knights. By ancient decree, dontchaknow.
** '''Player:''' What? But I am a member of the White Knights! I went and did that stuff with the cabbage and the potion!
** '''Player:''' And I've already done your stupid little mind test thing! You can't get rid of me that easily!
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:'''Calm yourself down [lad/lass]... We have no intention of 'getting rid of you', we are very keen on seeing you join us...
** '''Player:''' Then what's the problem?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well, it's like this: Although you have performed a mission on behalf of the White Knights, you were never actually working for them.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' As I say, when our order was founded, we were expected to take the best of the best, and it was assumed that anyone worth having on the team would have been a White Knight already, what?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Sadly, this is not the case anymore, and the fact that you have to serve as a Squire to a White Knight for a minimum period of five years before you can actually become one tends to put the more short-attention spanned adventurers off joining, what?
* '''Player:''' You want me to be a squire for five years?!?!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Haw Haw Haw! No, no, goodness me, no!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Five years is a very long period of time nowadays – ANYTHING could happen in that time, and we need capable [men/women] like yourself as soon as possible.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' No, I had a bit of a chat with Sir Vey, and we came up with... A different solution to the problem. Assuming you're still interested, of course.
* {{topt|Yes I'm interested.}}
** '''Player:''' Yes I'm interested.
** '''Player:''' I'm still up for joining the Temple Knights, I just don't really feel like spending five years running around shining peoples{{sic}} boots for them.
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Haw Haw Haw!
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' I quite understand, what? No, don't worry, we were doing a little research into the White Knight Charter, and found an interesting loophole, what?
** '''Player:''' Loophole? What kind of loophole?
** '''Player:''' Well, you don't need to worry about the details, you just do as I tell you and it will all work out for us both, what?
** '''Player:''' I guess... I don't really like blindly following orders though...
** {{tact|end}}
* {{topt|No, I'm bored now.}}
** '''Player:''' No, I'm bored now.
** '''Player:''' No, to be honest I'm getting sick of all these excuses and procrastination. Plus being an initiate was nowhere near as fun as I thought it would be.
** '''Player:''' I'd rather just go off by myself and kill stuff for cash.
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well, sorry to hear you say that [boy/gal/bean], but I can't hold it against you.
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' You will of course still be entitled to all that we can provide you with at an initiate level, but sadly you will never advance beyond that in our order. Was there anything you wanted?
** {{tact|(Shows standard dialogue.)}}

====When talking to Sir Tiffy again====
* {{topt|Ask about the Wanted! Quest}}
** '''Player:''' So what exactly do you want me to do?
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well now, it's really very simple. I want you to go to Sir Amik, tell him that you have decided to not join the Temple Knights, and that you have decided to become a White Knight instead.
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' He will then offer you to join as a squire; you will then decline his offer, and ask if you can be deputised instead.
** '''Player:''' Okaaaay....
** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' If you run into any problems with that, come and see me.
** {{topt|How will all that help?}}
*** '''Player:''' How will all that help?
*** '''Player:''' I don't exactly understand how that will help me become a White Knight...
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Ah, your curiosity does you credit old boy! Let me tell you a little history lesson.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Many centuries ago, the founders of the White Knights realized that in times of crisis, a long waiting period for new Knights could be disastrous for the kingdom, so they added a clause to the founding charter that would
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' allow any citizen who has performed an act in the name of the White Knight to become deputised on a purely temporary basis to a full-fledged White Knight.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well now, your actions in the Black Knights' Fortress on behalf of the White Knights make you eligible to be deputised.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Besides, you passed the trials to become a Temple Knight, what? If you're good enough to join the elite, then we'd not hesitate to deputise you into the White Knights.
*** '''Player:''' But if deputisation's only temporary, and I have to be a 'proper' White Knight to then become a Temple Knight, doesn't that mean that I am still going to have to squire for Amik for five years?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Ah, well this is where the beauty of the plan lies, dontchaknow?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Once you have been deputised, for the time that you are still working for their order, you are subject to all of the same rules and regulations that normal White Knights are, which means you are also eligible for battlefield
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' promotions.
*** '''Player:''' I... don't understand...
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Let me put it like this; As a deputy you will be considered the lowest possible rank of the White Knight, and may be removed entirely from their order at a whim.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' However, should you achieve some great feat while serving as a White Knight that you would be eligible for a promotion from, then by their own rules you would then have to be raised to a higher level within their order, and
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' it would therefore become impossible to remove your rank, or make you no longer a deputy.
*** '''Player:''' So I get to be a White Knight without having to do five years of pointless busywork?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Indeed. We Temple Knights have our reputation for a reason, donchaknow?
*** '''Player:''' Okay, I guess that makes sense... But we're not in a time of crisis, so doesn't that mean I won't actually get deputised?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Ah, do not concern yourself with THAT old [boy/gal]; Crisis is such a subjective term, and as you will find out when you join us fully in the Temple Knights, is always some potential crisis or other brewing.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' If we weren't as good at our jobs we were, almost every day would be a day of crisis!
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' All we really need to do is allow the old White Knights to handle a problem that we would normally deal with, and that should be a sufficient crisis to allow you to be deputised, donchaknow.
*** {{tselect|Select an Option}}
*** {{topt|Do I really have to quit the Temple Knights?}}
**** {{tact|below}}
*** {{topt|What am I supposed to be doing again?}}
**** {{tact|below}}
** {{topt|Do I really have to quit the Temple Knights?}}
*** '''Player:''' But... Do I really need to quit the Temple Knights
*** '''Player:''' You seem pretty cool, and I really like the armour...
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Haw Haw Haw!
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' No, not at all, this is merely a ruse; Sir Amik knows full well that his authority over our organization is limited, so he will not deputise you unless he thinks that you are entirely unaffiliated with us.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' You are simply going to tell him that you have decided not to join us so that he will in turn deputise you to join the White Knights! We of course still want you to join us, it is just a
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' little... misdirection.
*** {{tselect|Select an Option}}
*** {{topt|How will all that help?}}
**** {{tact|above}}
*** {{topt|What am I supposed to be doing again?}}
**** {{tact|below}}
** {{topt|What am I supposed to be doing again?}}
*** '''Player:''' What am I supposed to be doing again?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' You are to go to Sir Amik Varze, leader of the White Knights of Falador, and are to tell him that you have decided not to join the Temple Knights.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' We will send him a messenger confirming this. He will offer you the opportunity to become his squire, you are to decline, and ask if you can be deputised instead.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' If you run into any problems with that, you are then to come and see me for further advice.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Understood?
*** '''Player:''' Um, yeah, I think so...
*** {{tact|end}}

==Falador Castle: First visit to Sir Amik==
* '''Player:''' Hello Sir. Amik
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Hello Friend
* {{tcond|If the player has not asked Tiffy about his plan:}}
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Well, I did have something you may have been interested in, but I've been given orders not to approach you for trivial matters now that you are a member of the Temple Knights.
** '''Player:''' Aaaaawwwww...
** {{tact|end}}
* '''Player:''' got any jobs for me to do today?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Well, I did have something you may be interested in, but I've been given orders not to-
* '''Player:''' Can I just stop you right there?
* '''Player:''' I am no longer a member of the Temple Knights, I have entirely renounced my application because after many hours of soul searching I have come to the conclusion that I desperately want to become a White Knight instead!
* {{tcond|If the player is wearing Initiate Armour:}}
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' You don't want to be a Temple Knight anymore?
** '''Player:''' That is correct, yes.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' But you are wearing their armour!
** '''Player:''' So? Did you hear me bad mouth their armour? The armour is cool, I kept the armour!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' I'm not sure I understand... You wish to desert the Temple Knights in favour of my own humble White Knights?
* '''Player:''' Absolutely!
* '''Player:''' And this is definitely not some kind of elaborate hoax to get my application processed faster.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' I'm sorry, did you say something just then?
* '''Player:''' No... Not if you didn't hear it...
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' ...Well anyway, for the fine work you achieved at that incident in the Black Knights Fortress, I would be honoured to accept you as my squire.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' So what do you say?
* {{tselect|Select an Option}}
* {{topt|What does being a Squire entail?}}
** '''Player:''' So what exactly does being a squire consist of, then?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' You will spend the next five years of your life running various errands for me.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' If I consider you to have done a more than adequate job of this, then I will begin to consider possibly moving your name forwards to becoming a full-fledged member of the Order of the White Knights.
** '''Player:''' So... No killing stuff for you, or anything fun like that...?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Not as such, no. Unless you consider killing time by running errands fun...?
** '''Player:''' Well, erm...
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' So what say you Player? Spend five long years of your life doing pointless busywork without pay for me, on the off chance that at some point in the future I may put your name forward as a novice White Knight?
** {{tact|other}}
* {{topt|Yes please!}}
** '''Player:''' Yes please! I would LOVE to be your squire!
** '''Player:''' Wait... Didn't Sir Tiffy tell me to decline...?
** '''Player:''' Nah, Squire me up, Amik!
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' As you wish, from this day forth you are my Squire!
** {{tact|end}}
* {{topt|No, not right now...}}
** '''Player:''' Um... As tempting an offer as that sounds Sir Amik, I am really not a fan of waiting around...
** '''Player:''' Now I heard from some completely random stranger that was definitely not Sir Tiffy Cashien as part of an elaborate ruse to fast track my application into the White Knights, that I could be deputised directly as a White
** '''Player:''' Knight, and entirely bypass that while boring waiting for five year thing...
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' That is entirely correct [player name], but I have no idea where you might have heard that for it is a secret guarded only to members of our order!
** '''Player:''' Right. Well, Can I do that instead?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Well, a fine adventurer would be a perfect candidate as a deputy in my eyes, but fortunately this can only be done in a time of crisis, and all is currently well.
** '''Player:''' All is well huh? I'll be right back, I just have to go ask...
** '''Player:''' nobody in particular if they can organise...nothing at all to happen.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' You are a strange one [player name]...
** {{tact|end}}

===Talking to Sir Amik after refusing to become his squire===
* '''Player:''' Hello Sir Amik.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Hello, friend!
* '''Player:''' Got any jobs for me to do today?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Only to become my Squire. Do you wish to become my squire?
* '''Player:''' No, not really. So there isn't a crisis yet?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Thank Saradomin, no! We have not had a crisis for many years now!
* '''Player:''' Right. Well, I'll go and see if Tiffy can fix that for me then.
* '''Player:''' Er, I mean: How fortunate for us all that nothing terrible has happened or is conveniently about to happen.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Indeed!
* {{tact|end}}

===Talking to Sir Amik after becoming his squire===
* '''Player:''' Hello Sir. Amik
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Hello Friend
* '''Player:''' So do you have any squiring for me today Amik?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Yes, yes in fact I do. I would like you to bring me an Asgarnian Ale.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' No matured Ale, no other brand, simply an Asgarnian Ale.
* '''Player:''' Okay boss.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' That is 'Sir Amik' or 'My Liege' – not 'Boss'.
* '''Player:''' Gotcha, boss.
* {{tact|end}}

===Returning with Asgarnian Ale===
* '''Player:''' Hello Sir. Amik
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Hello Friend
* '''Player:''' Hi boss.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Call me Sir Amik, or liege.
* '''Player:''' Sure thing, boss. I brought you a beer.
* '''Player:''' Anything else you want boss?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Well, another Asgarnian Ale would be greatly appreciated. And stop calling me Boss.
* '''Player:''' Sure thing boss!
* {{tact|end}}

==Falador Park: The Crisis==
===Talking to Sir Tiffy after agreeing to be Amik's Squire===
* '''Player:''' I did it Tiffy! I became Sir Amik's squire!
* '''Player:''' That means in just five short years I can be a White Knight!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' ...What? I told you to decline!
* '''Player:''' Yeeeesssssss.... But I thought it would be more fun to do this?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' *sigh* This is a less than illustrious start to your career with us [player name].
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Never mind, all is not lost, I have sent word to Amik that a dangerous criminal we once thought exiled is on the loose again, and that he should send all available White Knights to confront him.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' This should give him enough of a scare to deputise you immediately.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Go and speak to Amik again, and then immediately come back here, we will need to prepare you for your mission.
* {{tact|end}}

===Talking to Sir Tiffy after declining to be Amik's Squire===
* '''Player:''' Hello again Tiffy. I've just spoken to Amik, and he says he's fine for me to be deputised as a White Knight, there just needs to be a crisis first.
* '''Player:''' I assume you can arrange a small crisis for me?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Haw Haw Haw, fitting right in with the Temple Knight way of thinking, what?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well as a matter of fact, I will not have to organise anything, there is a particularly vicious and dangerous criminal currently at loose!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' I have already notified Sir Amik through the usual channels, so just go and speak to him quickly, then pop back here and we'll get you equipped for the hunt, what?
* '''Player:''' You know... Is all this back and forth strictly necessary?
* '''Player:''' I don't mean to sound ungrateful for the help you're giving me here, but you could have just got this sorted out before sending me off there in the first place!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Ah, yes, well most awfully sorry about all the running around you've been doing old bean, but when it comes to our line of work, sometimes the indirect route is the best, what?
* '''Player:''' I don't understand...?
* '''Player:''' And stop calling me bean! I am not a vegetable, I am a human bean, er, being!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Awfully sorry dear heart, it's just a term of affection, what? Anyway...
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' This criminal is a very dangerous fellow, and has some very dangerous friends.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' If he has no reason to suspect you to be working for us, then he will be a little sloppier than he otherwise might be, because he will just assume you to be one of those stupid White Knights.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' We want this man caught, for the good of the country, and if we need to fool a few people into thinking you're nothing to do with us, and you have to do a little extra walking, then that's what is going to happen, what?
* '''Player:''' I guess... But this spy stuff is a lot more boring than it sounded at first...
* {{tact|end}}

===Talking to Sir Tiffy before revisiting Sir Amik===
* '''Player:''' Hi Tiffy. What am I supposed to be doing again?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Go and report to Sir Amik for 'orders'. You will be unsurprised to find out he will now be prepared to deputise you.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' After you have spoken to him return here immediately, your life will be at risk without our assistance, and we don't want you dying on your first mission for us, what?
* {{tact|end}}

==Falador Castle: Second visit to Sir Amik==
* '''Player:''' Speak about the Wanted! Quest
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Thank Saradomin you have come back here!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' We have received word from one of our scouts that the evil Solus Dellagar has returned to Asgarnia!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' What is worse, it seems he is in possession of dangerous new magics we are powerless against!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Please, I implore you in the name of all that is holy, assist us in banishing him from our kingdom once more!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' I cannot bear to think of the troubles he may bring us...
* {{topt|Who is Solus?}}
** '''Player:''' Solus Dellagar? Who - or what - is that?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' You have not heard of the infamous murder-mage? He terrorised these lands many years past, thinking himself above the law with his powerful enchantments, but the White Knights finally managed to bring him to
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' justice and he was permanently exiled.
** '''Player:''' Exiled? Why didn't you just kill him? Or, you know, imprison him or whatever?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Kill an unarmed foe? That is not the way of Saradomin, nor of the White Knights.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' As for imprisonment...Solus is a highly dangerous man not just because of his mastery of the magical arts, but for his uncanny resourcefulness.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' To tell you the truth, I fear that I would be unable to guarantee his captivity in any holding cell belonging to us...
** '''Player:''' So if you can't imprison him, and exile obviously didn't work, you want me to kill him?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' I did not say that. I did not say that at all. That is against all of our codes of honour.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' However, should you find yourself in combat with him, and there is no other way, then perhaps for him to die would be best for us all.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' You understand me, [player name]?
** '''Player:''' Oh yes. You've made yourself very clear.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' So will you aid us in this fight?
** {{tact|other}}
* {{topt|What will I get for helping?}}
** '''Player:''' So what will I get if I help you out against this, er, solus doolalala guy then?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Why, you will help keep Asgarnia free of malicious influence, and perform a good deed in the name of King Vallance and of the White Knights!
** '''Player:''' Yes, yes I see. So...No monetary compensation or other actual reward then?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' As Saradomin has told us, 'a good deed is its own reward'.
** '''Player:''' So I don't actually get anything out of this then?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Nothing tangible, no, but you will be deputized to act as a White Knight, and you will feel a warm glow inside of having done the right thing.
** '''Player:''' But you can't buy things with a warm glow...
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' So what say you [player name]? Fight with honour in the name of the White Knights against the terrible Solus?
** {{tact|other}}
* {{topt|Sure I'll help you!}}
** '''Player:''' Hey, there's nothing I like more than fighting! Deputise me up, and I'll go get this guy for you!
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Very well then; In the name of King Vallance, and for the honour of Saradomin, I now pronounce you a fully deputised White Knight.
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' May honour and glory follow you in every action.
** '''Player:''' So where should I start looking for this Solus guy anyway?
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' I haven't the faintest idea. Good Luck!
** {{tact|end}}
* {{topt|I don't feel like helping you right now.}}
** '''Player:''' A scary mage huh? Sounds like I could get KILLED!
** '''Player:''' Forget that and forget you! No way am I risking my neck unless there is a big fat reward on the table for it!
** '''Player:''' And sometimes not even then...
** '''Sir Amik Varze:''' It is a tragedy to hear you speak so, but we must trust in our valour and the wisdom of Saradomin to protect us from this evil man if we can not enlist help against him.
** {{tact|end}}

===Talking to Sir Amik after agreeing to go after Solus===
* '''Player:''' Hello Sir Amik.
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' Hello, friend!
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' [Player]... Solus Dellagar is still loose... Please, for the safety of my lands you must find him immediately...
* '''Player:''' Any hints on where he might be?
* '''Sir Amik Varze:''' We have no idea where he might be now... His magic is too powerful for us, I do not know how to stop him...
* '''Player:''' Hum. I wonder if Sir Tiffy might be able to help?
* {{tact|end}}

==Falador Park: Mission details==
* '''Player:''' Tiffy, it worked exactly to plan! Sir Amik deputised me to take care of this Solus Doolally dude, so let's go get him!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' His name is Solus Dellagar, and honestly, he is one of the most dangerous men on the planet.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Not only is he extremely adapt at magic, he is also quite prepared to use that magic at a moments{{sic}} notice, because he is quite, quite mad.
* '''Player:''' So... Dragonhide armour and an elemental shield and I'm laughing?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' No, no, no. Listen to me [boy/girl]; You are under NO CIRCUMSTANCES to engage him directly in combat!
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' You are to act as our agent and track him down, when you have managed to corner him somewhere then we will send in a field recovery team directly to take him away without incident.
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' I cannot stress this enough; You are to observe and locate, you are NOT to enter a combat situation!
* '''Player:''' Riiiiight.... So I'm just going to scout him out for you then.
* '''Player:''' Any tips on how to find him?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well old [boy/girl], we need to equip you for the task, but you'll need to get something for me first, what?
* '''Player:''' What kind of stuff?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well, we teach our operatives the value of their equipment by making them pay for it, what?
* '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' You have two choices here, you can either give me the cash directly, and I will hand it over, or you can bring me the raw materials and I will make you one.
* {{topt|Buy One}}
** {{tcond|If the player does not have enough inventory space:}}
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Well we in the Temple Knights have some specific equipment for situations such as this dontchaknow, but you'll need to free up some inventory space first!
*** {{tact|end}}
** {{tcond|If the player has enough inventory space and enough money:}}
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' It's 10,000 coins for the Temple Knight Communication Orb. You have that kind of money with you?
*** '''Player:''' Oh yeah, I got your money.
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Then you wish to purchase a Temple Knight Communication Orb?
*** {{topt|Yes}}
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Here take this Communications Orb, or 'CommOrb' for short.
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' It is a precise magical mechanism that we can use to track him, by sending data of the surrounding area back to one of our information operatives at the Temple Knight Headquarters.
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Your information operative is known as Savant, I suggest you contact them using your CommOrb at your earliest convenience.
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' They will be able to send you any useful data about Solus directly through it for your perusal, dontchaknow.
**** '''Player:''' Ok, thanks Tiffy!
**** {{tact|end}}
*** {{topt|No}}
**** '''Player:''' Actually, no thanks.
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' As you wish, what?
**** {{tact|end}}
*** {{tcond|If the player has enough inventory space but not enough money:}}
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' It's 10,000 coins for the Temple Knight Communication Orb. You have that kind of money with you?
**** '''Player:''' I don't have that kind of money with me...
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Perhaps you would like to just bring me the components of it then, what?
**** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Bring me one law rune, one slayer gem and one piece of molten glass and I will make you a Communication Orb.
**** {{tcond|If the player has one of the items needed:}}
***** '''Player:''' Can I give you the components I have with me now?
***** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Heavens, boy! I am not a pawn shop, to be holding bits and pieces for you while you go off and do something else!
***** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' No, no you bring me all three components at the same time, what?
**** '''Player:''' Okay Tiffy.
**** {{tact|end}}
* {{topt|Have One Made}}
** {{tcond|If the player doesn't have the materials:}}
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Bring me one Law Rune, one Slayer Gem and one piece of Molten Glass and I will make you a Communication Orb.
** {{tcond|If the player has one of the items needed:}}
*** '''Player:''' Can I give you the components I have with me now?
*** '''Sir Tiffy Cashien:''' Heavens, boy! I am not a pawn shop, to be holding bits and pieces for you while you go off and do something else!

==CommOrb Files==
====Dossier of Solus Dellagar====
Solus Dellagar

'''Age:''' Unknown

'''Place of Birth:''' Unknown

'''Known aliases:'''

Solus Darkheart

The Murder-mage

'Mad Solus'

Stealthy Pete

'''Known affiliates:'''
Solus Dellagar has shown time and time again that he considers others to be mere pawns in his schemes, and will discard them at the first viable opportunity.

Despite having been associated with many criminal and undesirable elements known to us, these will not be listed as affiliates, because as a general rule, if you have worked with Solus once you are unlikely to ever do so again.

The only loyalty he has ever managed to inspire in others is through threat of force,
and he is indeed powerful, but the general hatred of him amongst those he has encountered means that an experienced field operative will find opportunities to turn associates against him.

'''Known History:'''
Solus' exact history is still unknown to us, but we expect he originated in or close to the city of Ardougne.

He was first brought to our attention as a possible threat in the infamous zombie attack of Varrock masterminded by the Mahjarrat Lucien, but did not serve a particularly key role.

Reports of his whereabouts after that are somewhat sketchy, but he seems to have trained a quite formidable magical aptitude in the decades immediately following.

Although he has kept a low profile from conventional authorities, he has made quite a name for himself amongst various underworld circles, especially amongst Zamorakians, where his particularly brutal methods have been cause for some concern.

He earned the nickname 'Murder-mage' and 'Mad Solus' in the incident at Edgeville where he was eventually apprehended by one of our field teams, but not before the willful slaughter of over 800 civilians, as well as an equal number of White and Black Knights – we are still unsure as to why Lord Daquarius allowed his men to be involved in this affair, because it was distinctly against his usual methods, and this is the only report on file of him making an action under someone else's direct command.

Solus' reputation as an evil, bloodthirsty, greedy and dangerous individual is well deserved, and field operatives should avoid direct confrontation at all costs.

Standard observation rules apply, and if you find yourself in a position where confrontation seems likely, then guidelines suggest you immediately contact your handler who will provide you ample backup – your chances of surviving a one on one assault are slim to none.

End of Report.

====Dossier of Lord Daquarius====
Lord Daquarius

'''Age:''' 34

'''Place of Birth:''' Falador

'''Known aliases:'''

Lord of the Kinshra

Daquarius Rennard

'''Known affiliates:'''

Leader of the Kinshra, commonly known as the Black Knights.

Has occasional ties to various other Zamorakian agencies.

Has been linked with the Witch Lensig and her familiar Greebo most recently.

Has a number of jail guards in his employ deep in the Taverley dungeon, where enemies of the Kinshra are often imprisoned.

'''Known History:'''

The charismatic Lord Daquarius was the youngest Black Knight to ever take lead of the Kinshra, being unanimously voted into leadership after the death of Lord Shadwell in the infamous incident at Edgeville.

As leader of the Kinshra, he has been a high priority threat to the White Knights, and his activities with the Black Knights have been a constant thorn in both the side of the White Knights and of the Temple Knights.

However, while the White Knights still actively seek his capture and execution for crimes against the state, the Temple Knights have taken a more reactive position of observation.

Those of us who survived the Edgeville incident and recall what the Black Knight's activities under Lord Shadwell were like, can see that Lord Daquarius is better to keep around than risk another fanatical extremist taking his place.

For all of his misguided belief in Zamorak, Daquarius is an undeniably noble man; we have numerous reports of him risking personal injury so that he may save a fellow Kinshra from harm or death.

His belief in his men as more than simply subjects to be used in war against the White Knights makes him a predictable and easily manipulated enemy that is worth keeping in place, and also accounts for the high regard in which he is held by the rest of the Kinshra.

End of report.

====Dossier of Zamorakian Research Mage====
Name Unknown

'''Age:''' Unknown

'''Place of Birth:''' Unknown

'''Known affiliates:'''

The Zamorakian Magical Institute, a research group that we have classified as fairly innocuous that primary focuses on magical research dedicated to assisting Zamorak.

Observation suggests that the Z.M.I. is the same faction of Zamorakians that burnt down the Wizards Tower in Operation: Phoenix, but firm conclusions as to the reason for this remain at best sketchy and unsupported by evidence.

It is certainly worthwhile to keep any known members of the Z.M.I. under observation, however.

'''Known History:'''

Recently this unidentified mage has begun to base his activities in and around the area of Varrock, specifically the Temple to Zamorak that was foolishly allowed to be built there.

We have some evidence that this Mage also works as a rune-dealer in the vicinity of Edgeville, but preventing his operations in the Wilderness is far beyond our jurisdiction, and any actions taken there would bring unwanted attention to our order by various entities.

We have installed standard observation devices in the Zamorakian temple, but his activities have apparently been limited to a small degree of smuggling and the purchasing and resale of various pieces of contraband.

Although no obvious threat from his presence has been detected, we will continue standard observation in case he turns out to be a bigger fish than he at first glance seems.

End of report.

* '''Savant:''' It is far too dangerous. In my example I only used two places, but in practice there is a third place involved. It is a strange, empty dimension that teleports seem to pass through.
* '''Savant:''' Frankly, the human mind isn't equipped to cope with processing so much information simultaneously, and the test subjects started to display... behavioral problems...
* '''Player:''' Er... That doesn't sound good...
* '''Savant:''' No, it wasn't: Aggressiveness was highly increased, and there were numerous perceptual faults, hallucinations and the like.
* '''Savant:''' We ceased experimentation immediately, there are enough things in this world to haunt a Temple Knight, we did not see the need to create anymore.
* '''Player:''' But this signature you are finding is the same as this 'slow-teleport' experiment created?
* '''Savant:''' No... it is not exactly the same...
* '''Savant:''' But it is close enough to be a cause of concern.
* '''Savant:''' Either someone else has found this strange dimension and begun their own research, and reached the same conclusions that we did...
* '''Savant:''' Or... We have had a security leak within the Temple Knights and abandoned research has been sold to our enemies!
* '''Player:''' Well, I can see how that might be a cause for concern, but right now our priority is Solus, right?
* '''Savant:''' Yes [player name], you're right. But if he is using this 'slow-teleport' technique, then that means it is going to be a lot harder to catch him!
* '''Player:''' Because he is invisible and in three separate places simultaneously?
* '''Savant:''' Exactly. Listen, I might be able to block his teleportation magics using your CommOrb, but I will need you to get close to him, and if we're unlucky then he will end up teleporting to the point away from you instead of appearing in front of you {{sic}}
* '''Player:''' Well, we have nothing else to try, right? What will I have to do?
* '''Savant:''' Use the 'Scan' function of your CommOrb, if he is in the vicinity it should knock him out of his 'in-between' state, and make him appear.
* '''Player:''' So I should try a scan here in Canifis?
* '''Savant:''' That would seem best... Savant over and out.
* {{tact|end}}
